name: Cloud Custodian (Cloud Governance Policy Engine)

auth.notes: Cloud provider credentials (AWS profile, GCP SA, Azure SP)

performance.notes: Cloud API-dependent; large accounts take minutes per policy

agent_readiness.notes: YAML-policy-based cloud governance engine. custodian run -s out/ policy.yml. Filters and actions on cloud resources: EC2, S3, IAM, RDS, Lambda. Example: find unencrypted S3 buckets → tag them → notify Slack → encrypt or delete. Supports AWS, GCP, Azure, Kubernetes. Dry-run mode. CloudWatch Events/Lambda for real-time enforcement. Serverless deployment mode. CNCF graduated project. Used by enterprises for compliance, cost optimization, security enforcement.

gotchas: YAML policy DSL has steep learning curve
Actions can be destructive — always dry-run first
Large accounts require careful policy scoping
Documentation gaps for newer cloud services
best_when: Enterprise cloud governance and compliance enforcement
Real-time cloud resource compliance via Lambda triggers
avoid_when: Teams without YAML policy DSL familiarity
Small accounts where manual review is sufficient
summary: Cloud Custodian is a YAML-based cloud governance engine (CNCF project). Policies filter cloud resources then take actions (tag, notify, encrypt, stop, delete). AWS/GCP/Azure/K8s. Dry-run mode. Lambda mode for real-time enforcement. Used for compliance, cost optimization, security remediation. Steep learning curve but powerful for enterprise cloud governance.
